title: ObjectVisibility Object
description: An object that provides control over which objects are displayed in the graphics window. This is the equivalent of the "Object Visibility" settings in the Display Settings drop-down in the navigation toolbar at the bottom of the Fusion graphics window.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: isAllObjectsVisible
properties.description: Sets if all objects are visible or hidden. When setting this property it will set the value of all other properties of this object to true or false. When getting this property, if it is true, then all other properties are true. If false, one or more other properties are false.

properties.name: isJointOriginAxesVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the axes lines shown in the glyphs for joint origins in all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isJointOriginsVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the glyphs for joint origins in all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isJointsVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the glyphs for joints in all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible.

properties.name: isOriginAxesVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the origin construction axes of all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isOriginPlanesVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the origin construction planes of all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isOriginPointsVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the origin construction points of all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isSketchesVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the sketches in all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isUserWorkAxesVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the user created construction axes of all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isUserWorkPlanesVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the user created construction planes of all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isUserWorkPointsVisible
properties.description: Gets and sets if the user created construction points of all components, local or referenced by this design, are visible in the graphics window.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: ObjectVisibility.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

isAllObjectsVisible.title: ObjectVisibility.isAllObjectsVisible Property
isAllObjectsVisible.description: Sets if all objects are visible or hidden. When setting this property it will set the value of all other properties of this object to true or false. When getting this property, if it is true, then all other properties are true. If false, one or more other properties are false.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = objectVisibility_var.isAllObjectsVisible

# Set the value of the property.
objectVisibility_var.isAllObjectsVisible = propertyValue
